audit_items.proof_trace: ldif_open_mem calls ber_memalloc at L753 but, unlike the analogous ldif_open function (L731-734), does not check for NULL before dereferencing lfp at L755 (lfp->fp = fp); if the allocation fails under OOM conditions this is a null pointer dereference crash. All other functions in the file that allocate memory (ldif_open, ldif_parse_line2, ldif_read_record, ldif_must_b64_encode_register) correctly guard their allocations, making this an isolated omission. The function is exported in ldap.map and ldif.h as a public API but has no internal callers in this codebase.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An application using libldap directly that calls ldif_open_mem under memory pressure (OOM) will crash at L755 with a null pointer dereference, enabling a denial-of-service against any process that uses this API entry point.
